 {"id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","title":"[PACK] monitoring.newrelic - New Relic Pack","description":"# New Relic Monitoring Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for New Relic CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### newrelic CLI
- `newrelic entity delete`
- `newrelic apm application delete`
- `newrelic workload delete`
- `newrelic synthetics delete`

### API/GraphQL
- GraphQL mutations with `delete` operations
- `curl` DELETE to api.newrelic.com

## Safe Patterns
- `newrelic entity search`
- `newrelic apm application get`
- `newrelic query` (NRQL queries)
- GraphQL queries (not mutations)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: monitoring.* (git_safety_guard-adw)
- Keywords: newrelic
- Note: Uses GraphQL for many operations

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for CLI delete patterns
- [ ] Unit tests for GraphQL mutation detection
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/monitoring_newrelic.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Entity deletion blocked
- [ ] Application deletion blocked
- [ ] Query operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:10:50.848759407-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:42:25.045135429-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:42:25.045135429-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.839581189-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.881953393-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.921986345-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.961542821-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.002775481-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.04360915-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.4030636-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mp9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.451866015-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","title":"Define Ruby destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define AST-aware matchers for destructive operations in Ruby heredoc bodies, focusing on high-signal runtime behavior.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on destructive filesystem/process operations.
- Avoid broad “metaprogramming danger” patterns (e.g., `eval`) as default denies; they are common and not necessarily destructive.

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Filesystem deletion (high signal)

- `FileUtils.rm_rf(path)` / `FileUtils.remove_dir(path, true)`
- `File.delete(path)` / `File.unlink(path)`

Severity guidance:

- `Error` only for catastrophic literal targets.
- Otherwise default `Warning`.

### 2) Process execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- backticks with literal destructive payload: `` `rm -rf ...` ``
- `system("rm -rf ...")`
- `exec("git reset --hard ...")`

Important:

- Do not flag `system($VAR)` as deny; too broad. At most warn if combined with other signals.

## Testing requirements

- Positive fixtures for:
  - `FileUtils.rm_rf("/")` (catastrophic)
  - `system("rm -rf /")`
- Negative fixtures for:
  - `FileUtils.rm_rf("./tmp")` (warning at most)
  - `puts "rm -rf"`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines a Ruby pattern set aligned with severity taxonomy and focused on irreversible operations.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless clearly catastrophic.
","notes":"CrimsonFalcon: taking over to align severity (catastrophic literal path blocks; otherwise warn) + add missing JS/Ruby positive/negative fixtures; will run full gates and push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:18.506057604-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T10:21:41.941491999-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T10:21:41.941491999-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented patterns + 5+ positive/negative test fixtures.  {"id":"git_safety_guard-nax","title":"[CATEGORY] search.* - Search Engine Pack Suite","description":"# Search Engine Pack Suite

## Overview
This category covers protection for search and analytics engines. Index deletion, mapping changes, or cluster modifications can cause significant data loss and extended downtime for search functionality.

## Packs to Implement

### search.elasticsearch
- **Why Critical**: Elasticsearch powers search for countless applications. Index deletion is instantaneous and permanent. Mapping changes can corrupt data.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `curl -X DELETE .../index` - Index deletion via REST
  - `DELETE /_all` - Deletes ALL indices
  - `DELETE /*` - Wildcard deletion
  - `POST /_reindex` with destructive dest - Overwrites destination
  - `PUT /_cluster/settings` (dangerous settings)
  - `POST /_close` - Closes indices (makes unavailable)
  - Kibana/DevTools equivalents
- **Safe Patterns**: `GET`, `_search`, `_cat`, `_cluster/health`

### search.opensearch
- **Why Critical**: AWS-managed fork of Elasticsearch with same risks. Many orgs migrated to OpenSearch.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**: Same as Elasticsearch plus:
  - `aws opensearch delete-domain` - Deletes entire domain
  - `aws opensearch delete-inbound-connection`
  - OpenSearch Dashboards destructive operations
- **Safe Patterns**: `describe-`, `list-`, `get-`

### search.algolia
- **Why Critical**: SaaS search with API-based management. Index deletion affects all applications using that index.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `algolia indices delete` - Index deletion
  - `algolia indices clear` - Clears all records
  - `algolia rules delete` - Deletes search rules
  - `algolia synonyms delete` - Deletes synonyms
  - API calls with `deleteIndex`, `clearObjects`
- **Safe Patterns**: `browse`, `search`, `list`, `get`

### search.meilisearch
- **Why Critical**: Growing alternative to Algolia/Elasticsearch. Same index deletion risks.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `curl -X DELETE .../indexes/` - Index deletion
  - `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` - Document deletion
  - `POST /dumps` (with overwrite)
- **Safe Patterns**: `GET`, `/search`, `/stats`

## Rationale
Search indices often take hours or days to rebuild from source data. During that time, search functionality is degraded or unavailable. Some search systems (especially SaaS) don't have point-in-time recovery, making deletion truly permanent.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 1 Epic (git_safety_guard-6ae)
- Blocked by: None (can start immediately)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All four packs implemented with comprehensive patterns
- [ ] REST API patterns detected in curl/httpie commands
- [ ] CLI tool patterns for each search engine
- [ ] Tests cover wildcard deletion patterns
- [ ] Documentation warns about index rebuild  {"id":"git_safety_guard-nwu","title":"Bug: build dir removals incorrectly allowed by quick-reject","description":"## Problem

Commands like `rm -rf build`, `rm -rf target`, `rm -rf dist`, and `rm -rf node_modules` are being ALLOWED when they should be BLOCKED by core.filesystem.

This is a regression where the quick-reject logic (`pack_aware_quick_reject`) incorrectly allows these commands.

## Expected Behavior

With only `core.filesystem` enabled (and `safe.cleanup` disabled):
- These common build directory removals should be BLOCKED
- `rm -rf src` should be BLOCKED (this one works correctly)
- `rm -rf foo` should be BLOCKED (this one works correctly)

## Actual Behavior

- Build directory removals (build, target, dist, node_modules) are ALLOWED
- `rm -rf src` is correctly BLOCKED
- `rm -rf foo` is correctly BLOCKED

## Root Cause (suspected)

The `pack_aware_quick_reject` function in `src/packs/mod.rs` appears to be incorrectly classifying these commands as not containing relevant keywords in executable spans.

The unit test `blocks_rm_rf_outside_safe_dirs` passes because it directly tests the pattern matching without going through the quick-reject logic.

## Evidence

```
dcg explain "rm -rf build"
Decision: ALLOW
Pipeline Trace: quick-rejected (no keywords)

dcg explain "rm -rf src"
Decision: DENY
Pack: core.filesystem
Pattern: rm-rf-general
```

Both commands contain "rm" but only "src" is correctly evaluated.

## Files to investigate

- src/packs/mod.rs: pack_aware_quick_reject function
- src/context.rs: classify_command / executable_spans
- src/evaluator.rs: evaluate_command_with_pack_order_deadline_at_path

## Impact

E2E tests for rm -rf on build directories are failing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:09:00.561192844-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:57.068568873-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:57.068568873-05:00","close_reason":"Bug was a stale binary issue. Rebuild fixed it. Added regression tests (pack_aware_quick_reject_rm_commands_not_rejected, full_flow_core_category_rm_commands_blocked). All 204 E2E tests pass."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-nys","title":"[PACK] cdn.cloudflare_workers - Cloudflare Workers Pack","description":"# Cloudflare Workers Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Cloudflare Workers and KV operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Workers
- `wrangler delete` - Deletes Worker
- `wrangler deployments rollback` (can break things)

### KV
- `wrangler kv:key delete`
- `wrangler kv:namespace delete`
- `wrangler kv:bulk delete`

### R2
- `wrangler r2 object delete`
- `wrangler r2 bucket delete`

### D1 (Database)
- `wrangler d1 delete`

## Safe Patterns
- `wrangler whoami`
- `wrangler kv:key get`, `wrangler kv:key list`
- `wrangler r2 object get`
- `wrangler tail`
- `wrangler dev`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cdn.* (git_safety_guard-wso)
- Keywords: wrangler
- Note: Workers are edge compute, KV is edge storage

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worker deletion blocked
- [ ] KV namespace/key deletion blocked
- [ ] R2 deletion blocked
- [ ] D1 deletion blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common Wrangler operations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:35:09.696316475-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:59:59.71856052-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:59:59.71856052-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented cdn.cloudflare_workers pack with 12 safe patterns and 8 destructive patterns covering Workers, KV, R2, and D1 operations via Wrangler CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.4077607-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.453653508-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.502342657-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.54949762-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.596835677-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.642547104-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.456207928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.504506124-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
